name: Safe synthetic playbook: HIPAA access-control failure signal
description: Synthetic scenario where a lab EHR API role grants excessive read scope relative to HIPAA minimum necessary. Discusses segregation and auditing—no PHI payloads, identifiers are hashed fixtures.

simulated_timeline.synthetic_narrative: Periodic lab review compares clinical app roles against minimum-necessary matrices.
Role LAB_ANALYST_EXTENDED granted global read facet in sandbox tenant (metadata).
Access removal simulation and audit sampling plan drafted.

payload.title: LAB: Over-broad PHI API role (synthetic, no payloads)

events_emitted.headline: Access-control gap finding
events_emitted.synthetic_detail: Matrix comparison only; patient narratives never included.

events_emitted.headline: Compliance monitoring alert
events_emitted.synthetic_detail: Workflow opens privacy office review queue (simulation).

expected_agents_triggered.triggered_reason_synthetic: HIPAA-aligned access matrix breach requires policy agent.
High-severity access drift may map to privacy incident triage path.

expected_remediation.summary: Tighten role scopes, schedule user access re-certification, expand audit sampling on API calls (simulation).

expected_autonomous_remediation.synthetic_action: Attach role-to-function justification template to ticket (stub).

expected_human_approval_gates.synthetic_rationale: Determine if breach notification criteria evaluated (lab-only decision tree).

expected_final_report.title: HIPAA access-control failure — synthetic playbook outcome
expected_final_report.synthetic_executive_summary: A lab role exceeded minimum-necessary expectations; remediation centers on scope reduction and governance evidence, not real PHI handling.
